title: galium papillosum subsp. helodes
intro: Galium papillosum subsp. helodes, also known as the marsh bedstraw, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing pain. The health benefits of Galium papillosum subsp. helodes include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Galium papillosum subsp. helodes is typically prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
